DISCOVERY 302, ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S NOTICE MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY TO DEPOSE PLAINTIFF'S TREATING PHYSICIAN, DR. YOSS IS GRANTED. WHILE THE CITY DID NOT CONDUCT THE DEPOSITION BEFORE THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF, THE MOVING PAPERS ESTABLISH EXCUSABLE NEGLECT, AND THE LAW FAVORS TRIAL ON THE MERITS. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFFS WILL INCUR LITTLE OR NO PREJUDICE BY THE CONDUCTING OF THIS TWO HOUR DEPOSITION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. PRO TEM JUDGE: DAVID MCDONALD, NOT REPORTED.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-15-2012 Continued To Sep-17-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Of Reopen Discovery To Depose Plaintiff'S Treating Physician, Dr. Yoss
Off Calendar Apr-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lauren M. Monson, DCA, from Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3856, for defendant and moving party
Enrique Pearce, Law Offices, 415-434-9000, for plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and tentative ruling is adopted.
Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Notice Motion to Reopen Discovery To Depose Plaintiff'S Treating Physician, Dr. Yoss is granted. While the City did not conduct the deposition before the discovery cutoff, the moving papers establish excusable neglect, and the law favors trial on the merits. Moreover, Plaintiffs will incur little or no prejudice by the conducting of this two hour deposition. Order signed in open court. Pro Tem Judge: David McDonald, not reported.

Notice Of Reopen Discovery To Depose Pltf'S Treating Physician, Dr. Yoss

Off Calendar Jan-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S RE-NOTICE OF MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.
Appearances: Jonathan C. Rolnick of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3815) for defendant and moving party; Enrique Pearce and Matt Luton of the Pearce Law Offices (434.9000) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the second, third and seventh causes of action and denied as to the first and fourth causes of action. On the first cause of action for harassment based on disability, there are triable issue whether plaintiff is disabled and whether the conduct was severe and pervasive. On the second and third causes of action for disability discrimination and weight discrimination, defendant has met its burden that there is no discriminatory adverse employment action and plaintiff has not presented any evidence creating a triable issue. Plaintiff's evidence support claims for harassment, not discrimination. On the fourth cause of action for failure to prevent harassment there is a triable issue whether defendant responded appropriately. The seventh cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress lacks merit because plaintiff failed to comply with the government claims requirements. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077
